headline: Trump, revisionism and the citizens fighting for the truth
description: 'America 250' (11/13). The Trump administration wants to restore 'truth and sanity' to the national narrative. In response to this rewriting of the past, in which neither slavery nor the dispossession of Native American lands are given their rightful place, the 'pro-truth' camp is getting organized.

Iran and US negotiations stall over future of Strait of Hormuz
description: Despite assurances from Washington, negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz have not been smooth sailing. The possibility of an Iranian and Omani toll continues to divide policymakers, with some seeing it as the least worst option.

headline: Nearly 35,000 people became millionaires in France in 2025, UBS study finds
description: The number of millionaires worldwide has reached record highs, according to the report. Real estate and finance remain the most effective ways to build wealth.

headline: Paris Opera house renovations extended by three years
description: The iconic Palais Garnier will be closed from 2027-2032, three years longer than initially planned due to lead contamination.

headline: French taxman targets hot-air balloon sector
description: France's Finance Ministry accused hot-air balloon operators of applying a reduced VAT rate, reserved for air transport, instead of the higher rate for leisure activities. Business owners argue that the authorities changed the rules without warning.

description: For the past five days, in La Guaira, the area hit hardest by the two earthquakes, families of victims buried beneath collapsed buildings have worked without pause to pull relatives from the rubble.

headline: Lebanon's Shiites, torn over Hezbollah, question the meaning of their sacrifices
description: Days after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement, supporters and opponents of the militia-party are still measuring the consequences of the devastating war.

headline: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.
